inputs.prompt: Interview: Print interview with Sarah Webb, Technology Correspondent, The Daily Herald. Subject: Clearwater Analytics' data breach. Spokesperson: James Chen, CEO. Key messages: 1) The breach was identified by our own monitoring systems, not reported externally. 2) No evidence that any data was extracted or misused. 3) We have commissioned an independent cybersecurity audit and results will be published. Off-limits: Cannot name specific affected local authorities. Cannot discuss the specific vulnerability. Format: Phone interview, 20 minutes.

Interview: Local radio, 5 minutes live. Subject: New community garden opening. Spokesperson: Local MP. Key messages: 1) This is a community-led project. 2) It creates 20 volunteer positions. Off-limits: None.

Interview: TV interview with Anna Lindström, Health Correspondent, SVT (Swedish Television). Subject: Nordic Health Initiative clinical trial adverse reaction. Spokesperson: Dr. Ingrid Nørgaard, Executive Director. Key messages: 1) Patient safety is our absolute priority and the trial was paused immediately. 2) An independent review panel has been established and includes clinicians from outside NHI. 3) We have been fully transparent with regulatory agencies in all three countries. Off-limits: Cannot identify the patient, the hospital, or speculate on causation. Must not appear to pre-judge the review outcome. Format: Pre-recorded TV interview, 8 minutes. Previous statements: Holding statement issued yesterday is on record.

Interview: Live TV interview with Rachel Moore, senior investigative correspondent, Channel 6 News. Known for aggressive questioning and gotcha moments. Subject: Reports that the company's CEO was aware of safety concerns at the factory six months before the incident. Spokesperson: Catherine Lewis, VP Communications (not the CEO — the CEO has declined to appear). Key messages: 1) We are cooperating fully with the investigation. 2) We cannot comment on internal communications while the investigation is ongoing. 3) Our priority is supporting affected families. Off-limits: Must not speculate on cause, must not blame contractors, must not say anything that could be interpreted as admitting liability. Format: Live studio interview, 10 minutes, with a panel that includes a victims' advocate. The journalist has already published leaked internal emails.

Interview: Business segment on CNBC Africa with Samantha Mtshali. Subject: Ethiopian Airlines' expansion into drone delivery for medical supplies in rural East Africa. Spokesperson: Mekonnen Tadesse, COO. Key messages: 1) The programme has delivered 45,000 blood units to 2,500 clinics in its first year. 2) This is commercially viable, not charity — the logistics model scales. 3) Partnership with Rwanda's Zipline programme proves African-developed solutions work globally. Off-limits: Competitor comparisons with specific carriers. Cannot discuss military drone concerns. Format: Remote video interview, 12 minutes.

Interview: Podcast interview for The Policy Exchange, hosted by a former minister. Subject: The company's decision to leave a major industry trade association over its lobbying position on climate regulation. Spokesperson: CEO Sarah Park. Key messages: 1) We believe the trade association's position does not represent the majority of its members. 2) This is not an attack on the association — we respect their right to their position, but we can no longer lend our name to it. 3) We will continue to work constructively with regulators and industry peers. Off-limits: Cannot name other companies considering leaving. Cannot disclose internal trade association discussions. Format: Pre-recorded podcast, 30 minutes, but they will likely extract a 2-minute clip for social media — identify which quote they'll pull and prepare accordingly.
